vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2172: host-mode fresh respawn (pan start --fresh --host) never delivers kickoff — agent sits idle at ctx 0/out 0

plan.title: host-mode fresh respawn (pan start --fresh --host) never delivers kickoff — agent sits idle at ctx 0/out 0
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: pan start <id> --fresh --host --yes reports success and spawns a live tmux session, but the work agent never receives its kickoff and sits inert (ctx 0/out 0) indefinitely. Three verified root causes: (AC1) deliverInitialPromptWithRetry trusts deliverAgentMessage's ok:true, but the tmux fallback returns ok:true even when the paste did not land (tmux.ts:1185-1191) and the supervisor echo-confirm can fail on large briefs (PAN-2102) — there is no transcript-landing confirmation for the initial kickoff, unlike resumes. (AC2) start.ts:1373 calls spinner.succeed unconditionally and never inspects agent.kickoffDelivered; spawnAgent (agents.ts:3284-3294) records the failure but returns state silently for work agents. (AC3) a live-but-inert fresh start with kickoffDelivered===false is invisible to both nudge patrols: nudgeStalledResumeWorkAgents gates on lastResumeAt (deacon-auto-resume.ts:405) which a fresh start lacks, and nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads gates on isAgentIdleForNudge which returns false when runtime.json is absent (agent-idle.ts:9-11) — a never-kicked agent never fired a hook.
narratives.Proposal: Three independent, additive fixes — one per AC. (1) Add best-effort transcript user-record landing confirmation to deliverInitialPromptWithRetry, reusing captureTranscriptUserRecordSnapshot + waitForTranscriptUserRecordLanding already in delivery.ts; unconfirmed => {ok:false, failure:'kickoff-not-confirmed'}; confirmation only for claude-code/pi with known sessionId+workspace. (2) start.ts checks agent.kickoffDelivered===false and switches spinner.succeed -> spinner.fail + clear message + process.exitCode=1, preserving the session. (3) New deacon patrol redeliverUndeliveredKickoffs() (not gated on lastResumeAt/isAgentIdleForNudge) re-delivers initial-prompt.md to live kickoffDelivered===false work agents past a stuck threshold, and surfaces loudly (activity error + TTS + troubled) after repeated failure; wired into runPatrol().
narratives.NonGoals:
- Restoring or fixing the Docker workspace path (PAN-2170); host mode is the fallback this issue makes reliable.
- Adding PTY-supervisor wiring to Docker workspaces.
- Changing ctx-saturation detection (PAN-2169).
- Refactoring nudgeStalledResumeWorkAgents or nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads.
- Auto-killing, deep-wiping, or respawning inert agents — AC3 re-delivers or surfaces, never destroys recoverable state.
- Changing strike-role kickoff behavior (already throws + stops).

autoDecisions.summary: New deacon patrol rather than extending nudgeStalledResumeWorkAgents.
autoDecisions.rationale: The resume nudge has two gates (lastResumeAt, isAgentIdleForNudge) that assume a prior resume and a fired hook; bolting fresh-start kickoff handling onto it would muddy a working path. A dedicated additive patrol is the no-loss, surgical choice.

autoDecisions.summary: AC2 preserves the spawned session (no kill) and reports failure via non-zero exit.
autoDecisions.rationale: The live session is recoverable by AC3; killing it would defeat re-delivery. Loud report + exit code 1 satisfies 'no silent drop' without destroying recoverable state.

autoDecisions.summary: Transcript confirmation is best-effort, claude-code/pi only.
autoDecisions.rationale: codex/ohmypi use file-pointer kickoff; forcing transcript confirmation there would regress their startup. Guard on harness behavior + sessionId/workspace presence and default to confirmed when not confirmable.

autoDecisions.summary: Stuck threshold = 3 min; re-delivery cooldown = 10 min.
autoDecisions.rationale: 3 min is comfortably past the normal ready (up to 30s) + 2-attempt delivery window so the patrol never fires mid-flight; 10 min matches the neighboring STALLED_RESUME_NUDGE_COOLDOWN_MS.

items.title: Confirm initial-kickoff landing via transcript and report truthful failure (AC1)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents/delivery.ts (deliverInitialPromptWithRetry, ~:345), snapshot the workspace transcript BEFORE delivery (captureTranscriptUserRecordSnapshot), and after deliverAgentMessage returns ok, confirm a NEW user record landed (waitForTranscriptUserRecordLanding) before counting the attempt as success. On no-landing, let the existing 2-attempt loop retry; if still unconfirmed after the loop, return {ok:false, failure:'kickoff-not-confirmed'}. Confirmation is best-effort: run it only when the agent's sessionId and workspace are known AND the harness writes a confirmable transcript (claude-code/pi); for codex/ohmypi (file-pointer kickoff) skip confirmation and keep current ok-based behavior. Both helpers already exist in this file (used by deliverResumeMessageWithTranscriptConfirmation).

items.title: Given a claude-code work agent whose tmux/supervisor delivery returns ok:true but no user record lands in the transcript, deliverInitialPromptWithRetry returns ok:false with failure 'kickoff-not-confirmed' after exhausting its attempts (covered by a unit test with a stubbed snapshot/deliver).
Given delivery whose user record DOES land in the transcript, deliverInitialPromptWithRetry returns ok:true and the caller marks kickoffDelivered=true (existing path), with no extra retry.

Given a codex or ohmypi agent (file-pointer kickoff, or unknown sessionId/workspace), deliverInitialPromptWithRetry preserves current behavior: it does NOT run transcript confirmation and returns ok based on deliverAgentMessage alone (no regression — proven by a unit test).

Given a transcript that already contains a pre-existing user record before delivery, deliverInitialPromptWithRetry still produces ok:false when no NEW record lands — because it captures the before-snapshot prior to the first delivery attempt (proven by a unit test with a populated pre-snapshot).
pan start reports failure (non-zero exit) when the kickoff was not delivered (AC2)

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/start.ts (work-spawn branch, ~:1362-1428), after const agent = await spawnAgent(...), check if (agent.role === 'work' && agent.kickoffDelivered === false). When true: call spinner.fail(...) instead of spinner.succeed(...), print a complete operator message (the session spawned but the kickoff did not land; the agent is idle; the live session is PRESERVED; the deacon will retry within the stuck threshold or run `pan tell <id> "…"`), and set process.exitCode = 1. Do NOT kill the session. Leave the tracker/vBRIEF side effects (already executed in spawnAgent) intact; this bead changes only success-vs-failure reporting and the exit code.

items.title: Given spawnAgent returns a work AgentState with kickoffDelivered===false, the start command calls spinner.fail (not spinner.succeed) and sets process.exitCode=1 (proven by a unit/integration test that stubs spawnAgent).

On an undelivered kickoff the start command renders a failure message containing the agent id, the word 'kickoff', a statement that the session is preserved, and a `pan tell` pointer — and does NOT render the 'Beads: ✓' success lines.

Given spawnAgent returns kickoffDelivered===true, the start command preserves existing success output (spinner.succeed, Agent Details, Context Loaded) and exit code 0 — no behavior change on the happy path.
In the failure branch the start command preserves the spawned tmux session — it does not call stopAgent/kill — leaving it running for deacon recovery (a test asserts no termination call is made).
Deacon re-delivers undelivered kickoffs to live work-agent sessions, or surfaces them (AC3)

narrative.Action: Add exported async patrol redeliverUndeliveredKickoffs() to src/lib/cloister/deacon-auto-resume.ts, modeled on the existing nudge patrols but NOT gated on lastResumeAt or isAgentIdleForNudge. For each running role==='work' agent that is not paused/troubled, whose issue is open (isIssueClosed false), whose tmux session is alive (sessionExists), with state.kickoffDelivered===false, and Date.now()-Date.parse(state.startedAt) > KICKOFF_REDELIVERY_THRESHOLD_MS (new const, default 3*60*1000), guarded by a per-agent cooldown file .last-kickoff-redelivery (reuse the 10-min cooldown pattern): (1) read <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md; (2) re-deliver via deliverInitialPromptWithRetry; (3) on confirmed delivery set state.kickoffDelivered=true and save; (4) after a bounded number of consecutive failed re-deliveries, emit an activity error + TTS and mark the agent troubled so it is never silently inert. Wire it into runPatrol() in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts beside nudgeStalledResumeWorkAgents (~:2679) and re-export it from deacon.ts (~:143). Use async tmux primitives only and execAsync if shelling out; no execSync.

items.title: Given a running work agent with kickoffDelivered===false, a live tmux session, an open issue, and age since startedAt > 3 min, one patrol cycle re-delivers initial-prompt.md via deliverInitialPromptWithRetry and, on confirmed delivery, sets kickoffDelivered=true (proven by a unit test with injected deps + fake timers).

The patrol skips agents younger than KICKOFF_REDELIVERY_THRESHOLD_MS, paused/troubled agents, closed-issue agents, agents with no live tmux session, and agents with kickoffDelivered!==false — and respects the .last-kickoff-redelivery cooldown so it does not re-deliver twice within the cooldown window.

After the bounded number of consecutive failed re-deliveries, the patrol emits an activity error event and marks the agent troubled (verified by a test asserting the troubled transition and emitted event), so a persistently un-kickable agent is surfaced rather than left inert.

Each runPatrol() cycle emits the redeliverUndeliveredKickoffs action(s) into the patrol action log and deacon.ts re-exports redeliverUndeliveredKickoffs (a test stubbing the patrol deps asserts the action is emitted and the export resolves); delay-based tests use vi.useFakeTimers().
